metadata.summary: Jupyter AI magics Python package. Not published on NPM.

documentation:
# @jupyter-ai/magics

This is a Python library `jupyter_ai_magics` that exposes the model providers
and defines the IPython magics.

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.models.persona.Persona

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.models.persona.Persona.name
documentation:
Name of the persona, e.g. "Jupyternaut". This is used to render the name
shown on agent replies in the chat UI.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.models.persona.Persona.avatar_route
documentation:
The server route that should be used the avatar of this persona. This is
used to render the avatar shown on agent replies in the chat UI.

Model of an **agent persona**, a struct that includes the name & avatar
shown on agent replies in the chat UI.

Each persona is specific to a single provider, set on the `persona` field.

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.models.completion.InlineCompletionRequest
documentation:
Message send by client to request inline completions.

Prefix/suffix implementation is used to avoid the need for synchronising
the notebook state at every key press (subject to change in future).

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.models.completion.InlineCompletionItem
documentation:
The inline completion suggestion to be displayed on the frontend.

See JupyterLab `InlineCompletionItem` documentation for the details.

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.models.completion.InlineCompletionList
documentation: Reflection of JupyterLab's `IInlineCompletionList`.

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.models.completion.InlineCompletionReply
documentation: Message sent from model to client with the infill suggestions

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.models.completion.InlineCompletionStreamChunk
documentation: Message sent from model to client with the infill suggestions

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.completion_utils.token_from_request
documentation:
Generate a deterministic token (for matching streamed messages)
using request number and suggestion number

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.completion_utils.post_process_suggestion
documentation:
Remove spurious fragments from the suggestion.

While most models (especially instruct and infill models do not require
any pre-processing, some models such as gpt-4 which only have chat APIs
may require removing spurious fragments. This function uses heuristics
and request data to remove such fragments.

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.embedding_providers.BaseEmbeddingsProvider

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.embedding_providers.BaseEmbeddingsProvider.id
documentation: ID for this provider class.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.embedding_providers.BaseEmbeddingsProvider.name
documentation: User-facing name of this provider.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.embedding_providers.BaseEmbeddingsProvider.models
documentation:
List of supported models by their IDs. For registry providers, this will
be just ["*"].

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.embedding_providers.BaseEmbeddingsProvider.model_id_key
documentation: Kwarg expected by the upstream LangChain provider.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.embedding_providers.BaseEmbeddingsProvider.pypi_package_deps
documentation: List of PyPi package dependencies.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.embedding_providers.BaseEmbeddingsProvider.auth_strategy
documentation:
Authentication/authorization strategy. Declares what credentials are
required to use this model provider. Generally should not be `None`.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.embedding_providers.BaseEmbeddingsProvider.registry
documentation: Whether this provider is a registry provider.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.embedding_providers.BaseEmbeddingsProvider.fields
documentation:
Fields expected by this provider in its constructor. Each `Field` `f`
should be passed as a keyword argument, keyed by `f.key`.
Base class for embedding providers

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.utils.decompose_model_id
documentation: Breaks down a model ID into a two-tuple (provider_id, local_model_id). Returns (None, None) if indeterminate.

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.utils.get_lm_provider
documentation:
Gets a two-tuple (<local-model-id>, <provider-class>) specified by a
global model ID.

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.utils.get_em_provider
documentation:
Gets a two-tuple (<local-model-id>, <provider-class>) specified by a
global model ID.

modules.name: jupyter_ai_magics.tests.test_utils
documentation: Copyright (c) Jupyter Development Team.
Distributed under the terms of the Modified BSD License.

modules.name: jupyter_ai_magics.tests
documentation: Python unit tests for jupyter_ai_magics.

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.parsers.cell_magic_parser
documentation:
Invokes a language model identified by MODEL_ID, with the prompt being
contained in all lines after the first. Both local model IDs and global
model IDs (with the provider ID explicitly prefixed, followed by a colon)
are accepted.

To view available language models, please run `%ai list`.

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.parsers.line_magic_parser
documentation: Invokes a subcommand.

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.parsers.error_subparser
documentation:
Explains the most recent error. Takes the same options (except -r) as
the basic `%%ai` command.

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.parsers.version_subparser
documentation: Prints Jupyter-AI version

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.parsers.help_subparser
documentation: Show this message and exit.

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.parsers.list_subparser
documentation: List language models, optionally scoped to PROVIDER_ID.

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.parsers.register_subparser
documentation: Register a new alias called NAME for the model or chain named TARGET.

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.parsers.register_subparser
documentation: Delete an alias called NAME.

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.parsers.register_subparser
documentation: Update an alias called NAME to refer to the model or chain named TARGET.

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.parsers.register_subparser
documentation: Clear the conversation transcript.

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.parsers.ErrorArgs
documentation: Should match CellArgs

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.parsers.LineMagicGroup
documentation:
Helper class to print the help string for cell magics as well when
`%ai --help` is called.

functions.name: jupyter_ai_magics.tests.test_provider_metaclass.test_provider_metaclass
documentation:
Asserts that the metaclass prevents class attributes from being omitted due
to parent classes defining an instance field of the same name.

You can reproduce the original issue by removing the
`metaclass=ProviderMetaclass` argument from the definition of `Child`.

modules.name: jupyter_ai_magics._version
documentation: This file is auto-generated by Hatchling. As such, do not:
- modify
- track in version control e.g. be sure to add to .gitignore

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.EnvAuthStrategy

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.EnvAuthStrategy.name
documentation: The name of the environment variable, e.g. `'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'`.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.EnvAuthStrategy.keyword_param
documentation:
If unset (default), the authentication token is provided as a keyword
argument with the parameter equal to the environment variable name in
lowercase. If set to some string `k`, the authentication token will be
passed using the keyword parameter `k`.

Describes a provider that uses a single authentication token, which is
passed either as an environment variable or as a keyword argument.

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.MultiEnvAuthStrategy
documentation: Require multiple auth tokens via multiple environment variables.

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.AwsAuthStrategy
documentation: Require AWS authentication via Boto3

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.ProviderMetaclass
documentation:
A metaclass that ensures all class attributes defined inline within the
class definition are accessible and included in `Class.__dict__`.

This is necessary because Pydantic drops any ClassVars that are defined as
an instance field by a parent class, even if they are defined inline within
the class definition. We encountered this case when `langchain` added a
`name` attribute to a parent class shared by all `Provider`s, which caused
`Provider.name` to be inaccessible. See #558 for more info.

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.chat_models
documentation: Models which are suitable for chat.

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.completion_models
documentation: Models which are suitable for completions.

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider._call_in_executor
documentation:
Calls self._call() asynchronously in a separate thread for providers
without an async implementation. Requires the event loop to be running.

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider._generate_in_executor
documentation:
Calls self._generate() asynchronously in a separate thread for providers
without an async implementation. Requires the event loop to be running.

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.is_api_key_exc
documentation: Determine if the exception is an API key error. Can be implemented by subclasses.

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.update_prompt_template
documentation: Changes the class-level prompt template for a given format.

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.get_prompt_template
documentation:
Produce a prompt template suitable for use with a particular model, to
produce output in a desired format.

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.get_chat_prompt_template
documentation:
Produce a prompt template optimised for chat conversation.
The template should take two variables: history and input.

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.get_completion_prompt_template
documentation:
Produce a prompt template optimised for inline code or text completion.
The template should take variables: prefix, suffix, language, filename.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.id
documentation: ID for this provider class.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.name
documentation: User-facing name of this provider.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.models
documentation:
List of supported models by their IDs. For registry providers, this will
be just ["*"].

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.help
documentation:
Text to display in lieu of a model list for a registry provider that does
not provide a list of models.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.model_id_key
documentation: Kwarg expected by the upstream LangChain provider.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.model_id_label
documentation: Human-readable label of the model ID.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.pypi_package_deps
documentation: List of PyPi package dependencies.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.auth_strategy
documentation:
Authentication/authorization strategy. Declares what credentials are
required to use this model provider. Generally should not be `None`.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.registry
documentation: Whether this provider is a registry provider.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.fields
documentation:
User inputs expected by this provider when initializing it. Each `Field` `f`
should be passed in the constructor as a keyword argument, keyed by `f.key`.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.manages_history
documentation:
Whether this provider manages its own conversation history upstream. If
set to `True`, Jupyter AI will not pass the chat history to this provider
when invoked.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.persona
documentation:
The **persona** of this provider, a struct that defines the name and avatar
shown on agent replies in the chat UI. When set to `None`, `jupyter-ai` will
choose a default persona when rendering agent messages by this provider.

Because this field is set to `None` by default, `jupyter-ai` will render a
default persona for all providers that are included natively with the
`jupyter-ai` package. This field is reserved for Jupyter AI modules that
serve a custom provider and want to distinguish it in the chat UI.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.unsupported_slash_commands
documentation:
A set of slash commands unsupported by this provider. Unsupported slash
commands are not shown in the help message, and cannot be used while this
provider is selected.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.server_settings
documentation:
Settings passed on from jupyter-ai package.

The same server settings are shared between all providers.
Providers are not allowed to mutate this dictionary.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.prompt_templates
documentation:
Prompt templates for each output type. Can be overridden with
`update_prompt_template`. The function `prompt_template`, in the base class,
refers to this.

inner_classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.BaseProvider.Config
documentation: pydantic config

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.AI21Provider.is_api_key_exc
documentation: Determine if the exception is an AI21 API key error.

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.HfHubProvider

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.HfHubProvider._call
documentation:
Call out to Hugging Face Hub's inference endpoint.

Args:
    prompt: The prompt to pass into the model.
    stop: Optional list of stop words to use when generating.

Returns:
    The string or image generated by the model.

Example:
    .. code-block:: python

        response = hf("Tell me a joke.")
Handle text and image outputs
References for using HuggingFaceEndpoint and InferenceClient:
https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/main/en/package_reference/inference_client#huggingface_hub.InferenceClient
https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/blob/master/libs/community/langchain_community/llms/huggingface_endpoint.py

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.providers.QianfanProvider
documentation: Baidu QianfanChat provider. temporarily living as a separate class until

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.partner_providers.anthropic.ChatAnthropicProvider.is_api_key_exc
documentation: Determine if the exception is an Anthropic API key error.

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.partner_providers.gemini.GeminiProvider

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.partner_providers.openai.OpenAIProvider.is_api_key_exc

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.partner_providers.openai.ChatOpenAIProvider

https://platform.openai.com/docs/models/

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.partner_providers.aws.BedrockProvider

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.partner_providers.aws.BedrockChatProvider

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.partner_providers.aws.BedrockEmbeddingsProvider

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.partner_providers.aws.JsonContentHandler.replace_values
documentation: Replaces values of a dictionary recursively.

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.partner_providers.openrouter.OpenRouterProvider.validate_environment

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai_magics.partner_providers.openrouter.OpenRouterProvider.models

classes.name: jupyter_ai_magics.magics.FormatDict

Subclass of dict to be passed to str#format(). Suppresses KeyError and
leaves replacement field unchanged if replacement field is not associated

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.magics.AiMagics._ai_env_status_for_provider_markdown

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.magics.AiMagics._append_exchange

Appends a conversational exchange between user and an OpenAI Chat
model to a transcript that will be included in future exchanges.

methods.name: jupyter_ai_magics.magics.AiMagics._decompose_model_id
documentation: Breaks down a model ID into a two-tuple (provider_id, local_model_id). Returns (None, None) if indeterminate.
